description: The overdraft-floor policy in the REST transfer engine — the exact three-way logic (allow_overdraft=false→floor 0; allow_overdraft=true & credit_limit<0→unlimited; credit_limit>=0→floor -credit_limit; SYSTEM→unlimited) that CLAUDE.md flags as behaviour-equivalence-critical with the in-process LedgerServiceImpl and carries obvious historical-bug shape (PAR-319) — has no test. No test in the suite ever sets allow_overdraft or credit_limit (grep of src/test confirms zero occurrences outside the schema DDL), and the insertAccount seed helper (EmbeddedDbIT lines 49-57) omits those columns so they always default to allow_overdraft=0 / credit_limit=0.00. Only the default floor-0 INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS path is exercised (TransferAmountValidationIT line 115). A regression allowing overdraft debits below the credit-limit floor, or breaking SYSTEM's unlimited-sink behaviour, or drifting from the plugin engine, would pass CI green.
recommendation: Add integration tests seeding accounts with (allow_overdraft, credit_limit, account_type) permutations and asserting the floor: overdraft-disabled below 0 rejected; credit_limit=-1 sentinel debits past zero; credit_limit=N floors at -N; SYSTEM source debits unlimited. Extend EmbeddedDbIT.insertAccount (or add a variant) to set the overdraft columns.
rejection_reason: Snippet matches at TransferService.java:242-244, and it is true the TransferService IT suite never seeds allow_overdraft/credit_limit (grep confirms zero occurrences outside EmbeddedMariaDb DDL lines 123-124, and insertAccount at EmbeddedDbIT:49-56 omits both columns). BUT the finding's core claim -- that this behaviour-equivalence-critical three-way logic 'has no test' and 'a regression would pass CI green' -- is false. TransferService.java:243-244 does not implement the logic; it delegates one line to OverdraftPolicy.isWithinFloor in :common. That shared class (common/src/main/java/io/paradaux/common/OverdraftPolicy.java) is the single canonical rule both engines defer to, and it is covered by a dedicated, thorough unit test common/src/test/java/io/paradaux/common/OverdraftPolicyTest.java: it parameterizes all three floor branches (allow_overdraft=false floor 0, credit_limit<0 sentinel unlimited, credit_limit>=0 floor -credit_limit), the SYSTEM-always-unlimited invariant including the PAR-319 regression (lines 80-87, account #4 ChestShop System), and null credit_limit handling. Any regression to the floor/SYSTEM/credit-limit logic mutates OverdraftPolicy and is caught by common's tests -- it would NOT pass CI green. The engine-drift risk the finding invokes is precisely what OverdraftPolicy+its test exist to prevent. Premise materially wrong; reject.
